subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Region R Bounded by y=√x, y=6, y-axis — Area, Rotation Volume & Rectangular Cross Sections (Write Only)
stimulus: Let $R$ be the region in the first quadrant bounded by the graph of $y = \sqrt{x}$, the horizontal line $y = 6$, and the $y$-axis, as shown in the figure.

rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct integrand and limits: $\int_0^9 (6 - \sqrt{x})\, dx$ or equivalently $\int_0^6 y^2\, dy$... using the $x$-integral: $\int_0^9 (6 - x^{1/2})\, dx$

parts.prompt: Write, but do not evaluate, an integral expression that gives the volume of the solid generated when $R$ is rotated about the horizontal line $y = 7$.
rubric_criteria: 2 pts: Correct integrand using washer method: outer radius $= 7 - \sqrt{x}$, inner radius $= 7 - 6 = 1$; integrand: $\pi\left[(7 - \sqrt{x})^2 - (7-6)^2\right] = \pi\left[(7 - \sqrt{x})^2 - 1\right]$
1 pt: Correct limits and constant: $\pi\int_0^9 \left[(7 - \sqrt{x})^2 - 1^2\right]\, dx$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Volume $= \pi\int_0^9 \left[(7-\sqrt{x})^2 - (7-6)^2\right]\, dx = \pi\int_0^9\left[(7-\sqrt{x})^2 - 1\right]\, dx$ (do not evaluate).

parts.prompt: Region $R$ is the base of a solid. For each $y$, where $0 \le y \le 6$, the cross section of the solid taken perpendicular to the $y$-axis is a rectangle whose height is 3 times the length of its base in region $R$. Write, but do not evaluate, an integral expression that gives the volume of the solid.
rubric_criteria: 2 pts: Correct integrand: solving $y = \sqrt{x}$ for $x$ gives $x = y^2$; base of cross section in region $R = y^2$, height $= 3y^2$; cross-section area $= 3(y^2)^2 = 3y^4$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Solving $y = \sqrt{x}$ yields $x = y^2$. Each rectangular cross section at height $y$ has base $= y^2$ (length of base in $R$) and height $= 3y^2$, so area $= 3y^4$. Volume $= \int_0^6 3y^4\, dy$ (do not evaluate). Note: scoring key shows $\frac{3}{16}y^4$ based on $x = \frac{y^2}{4}$, consistent with the curve being rewritten with the region dimensions.
